source.description: Six-panel generated raster process sheet showing one parade drum with sticks from construction to finished sketch.
source.notes: Subject, pose, and final elements were locked before image generation for the July 3, 2026 current daily lesson.

process_strategy: The drum is built from one tilted cylinder construction with sticks, straps, and color introduced before the final cleanup pass.

final_elements.name: tilted drum cylinder
final_elements.description: A squat parade drum body with a visible top ellipse and lower curve.

final_elements.name: front rim and shell contour
final_elements.description: Clean top and bottom drum rims wrapping around the shell.

final_elements.name: crossed drumsticks
final_elements.description: Two slim sticks crossing above the drum at a clear angle.

final_elements.name: side straps and lacing
final_elements.description: Small strap and lace marks along the drum side.

final_elements.name: restrained red and blue accents
final_elements.description: Light red and blue colored-pencil accents on already drawn drum bands.

final_elements.name: soft ground shadow
final_elements.description: A pale shadow below the drum that grounds the object.

frames.visible_job: Draws the tilted top ellipse, lower curve, and center guide for the drum cylinder.
Turns the construction into a clean drum shell with top and bottom rims.
Adds two crossed drumsticks above the already placed drum.
Adds side straps, lace marks, and small rim details on the existing drum shell.
Adds restrained red and blue accents, light graphite shading, and a soft ground shadow to existing shapes.

final_step.visible_job: Darkens keeper contours, clarifies sticks, straps, lacing, accents, and shadow already present.

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, ink, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
